subject: Political Science
overall_learning_objectives: Critically analyse gender inequalities and their political implications
Evaluate the role of feminist movements in shaping social and political reforms
Examine the relationship between religion and politics in democratic societies
Assess the importance of secularism in maintaining equality and social harmony
Analyse the historical roots and modern transformation of the caste system
Evaluate the complex relationship between caste and electoral politics
Develop critical thinking about social divisions and their impact on democracy

lesson_plan.title: Understanding Social Diversity and Inequality
topics_covered: Difference between social difference and social division
Impact of social divisions on democracy
student_activities: Analyse examples of social diversity in different societies
Write short reflections on whether diversity strengthens democracy

lesson_plan.title: Gender Division and Patriarchal Structures
topics_covered: Gender division as a social construct
Patriarchy and its impact on women’s opportunities
teaching_strategy: Use of data showing gender inequality
student_activities: Conduct a mini survey of household labour division
Analyse how patriarchy influences social roles

lesson_plan.title: Feminist Movements and Women’s Political Representation
topics_covered: Demand for equal political and social rights
student_activities: Debate whether reservation for women in politics is necessary

lesson_plan.title: Religion, Politics and Communalism
topics_covered: Impact of communal politics on democracy
student_activities: Analyse examples of communal politics in history
Write short essays on dangers of communalism

lesson_plan.title: Secularism and Constitutional Provisions
teaching_strategy: Discussion on secular values in society
student_activities: Analyse case examples of religious equality

lesson_plan.title: Caste Inequalities and Social Reform
topics_covered: Role of social reformers such as Ambedkar, Phule, and Periyar
student_activities: Prepare comparative notes on social reformers
Discuss how reforms changed caste relations

lesson_plan.title: Caste and Electoral Politics
topics_covered: Role of caste in political mobilisation
Positive and negative effects of caste politics
student_activities: Analyse election strategies influenced by caste
Debate whether caste politics strengthens democracy

teaching_aids: Charts showing gender and caste statistics
teacher_tip: Encourage critical discussions and case study analysis so students understand how social divisions influence political systems and democratic governance.
